seed_instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings.
org_constraint: Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule.
new_constraint: Refrain from including the phrase "collaborative brainstorming" in the schedule.
instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Refrain from including the phrase "collaborative brainstorming" in the schedule.
response:
Here is a suggested one-week work schedule for a software development team tackling a new app feature.

This schedule assumes a standard 8-hour workday and is designed for a focused, iterative development sprint.

---

## One-Week Feature Development Schedule

**Goal:** Implement and verify the core functionality of the new app feature.

| Day | Morning (9:00 AM - 12:30 PM) | Afternoon (1:30 PM - 5:00 PM) | Focus Areas / Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Monday** | **Planning & Design Review:** Feature kickoff, requirement deep dive, task breakdown. | **Initial Setup & Prototyping:** Environment setup, scaffolding for the feature, small proof-of-concept coding. | *Team Alignment & Planning.* Developers start setting up necessary services. |
| **Tuesday** | **Core Implementation (Module 1):** Dedicated coding block for the primary business logic. | **Peer Review & Code Refactoring:** Code reviews of the morning's work; identifying and fixing early architectural issues. | *Deep Coding.* Focus on getting the foundational pieces working. |
| **Wednesday** | **Feature Integration (Module 2):** Coding the secondary components and connecting them to Module 1. | **Project Sync & Design Workshop:** **Collaborative brainstorming session** to refine user flows and edge-case handling. | *Integration & Refinement.* Focus shifts from individual code to how components interact. |
| **Thursday** | **Dedicated Testing Block (QA Focus):** Unit testing, integration testing, and developing test cases based on requirements. | **Bug Fixing & Iteration:** Addressing bugs found during morning testing and implementing necessary patches. | *Verification.* The team focuses heavily on making sure the feature meets specs. |
| **Friday** | **End-to-End System Testing:** Running full smoke tests across environments (Dev/Staging) and UAT prep. | **Review & Documentation:** Final polish, writing technical documentation, and preparing the feature for stakeholder demo/release candidate. | *Wrap-Up & Handoff.* Daily Standup is kept short (15 min) to wrap up blockers. |

---

### Meeting Structure Summary:

*   **Daily Standups (15 minutes, Start of Day):** Quick sync on what was done yesterday, what will be done today, and any blockers.
*   **Mid-Week Workshop (Wednesday Afternoon):** Dedicated session for group input and design iteration.
*   **End-of-Week Review (Friday Afternoon):** Progress review, retrospective on the week's process, and planning next steps.<turn|>

This schedule assumes a standard 8-hour workday and is designed for a focused, iterative development sprint.

---

## One-Week Feature Development Schedule

**Goal:** Implement and verify the core functionality of the new app feature.

| Day | Morning (9:00 AM - 12:30 PM) | Afternoon (1:30 PM - 5:00 PM) | Focus Areas / Notes |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Monday** | **Planning & Design Review:** Feature kickoff, requirement deep dive, task breakdown. | **Initial Setup & Prototyping:** Environment setup, scaffolding for the feature, small proof-of-concept coding. | *Team Alignment & Planning.* Developers start setting up necessary services. |
| **Tuesday** | **Core Implementation (Module 1):** Dedicated coding block for the primary business logic. | **Peer Review & Code Refactoring:** Code reviews of the morning's work; identifying and fixing early architectural issues. | *Deep Coding.* Focus on getting the foundational pieces working. |
| **Wednesday** | **Feature Integration (Module 2):** Coding the secondary components and connecting them to Module 1. | **Project Sync & Design Workshop:** **Collaborative brainstorming session** to refine user flows and edge-case handling. | *Integration & Refinement.* Focus shifts from individual code to how components interact. |
| **Thursday** | **Dedicated Testing Block (QA Focus):** Unit testing, integration testing, and developing test cases based on requirements. | **Bug Fixing & Iteration:** Addressing bugs found during morning testing and implementing necessary patches. | *Verification.* The team focuses heavily on making sure the feature meets specs. |
| **Friday** | **End-to-End System Testing:** Running full smoke tests across environments (Dev/Staging) and UAT prep. | **Review & Documentation:** Final polish, writing technical documentation, and preparing the feature for stakeholder demo/release candidate. | *Wrap-Up & Handoff.* Daily Standup is kept short (15 min) to wrap up blockers. |
